items.title: investingLive European markets wrap: US-Iran tensions continue; yen volatility in focus
items.summary: Headlines: US president Trump reaffirms that Iran has agreed to not have a nuclear weapon Iran reserves right to defend against any country permitting US attacks EU says latest US tariffs on forced labour grounds are unjustified USD/JPY continues to poke and prod at intervention strike zone BOJ governor Ueda says will continue to raise policy rate if baseline outlook holds ECB policymaker Elderson says prolonged war increases likelihood of second-round effects SNB Chairman Schlegel says medium-term inflation pressure is basically unchanged Eurozone business activity struggles further in May amid surging price pressures UK May final services PMI 49.3 vs 47.9 prelim How likely is a U.S. debt crisis? Markets: WTI crude up 2% to $95.70 European indices lower, DAX down 0.9% while CAC 40 down 0.4% S&P 500 futures down 0.1% USD a little higher, USD/JPY volatility swings after nearing 160 US 10-year yields up 2.8 bps to 4.48% Gold down 0.5% to $4,463 It was a more pensive session as we continue to wait on whether or not the US and Iran will strike a deal this week. But by the look of things, it seems that both sides are still finding it hard to meet in the middle especially on key terms. US president Trump came out to reaffirm that Iran has agreed to not have a nuclear weapon. But as a reminder, this notion of a baseline promise was denied by Tehran previously last week already. Besides that, he also said that the US naval blockade may stay the course until Labour Day. If so, that means it will be another three more months of this with the naval blockade being lifted supposed to be a key condition for Iran in this framework agreement. So, make what you will of that. Markets remain unfazed for the most part despite the mix of headlines. However, oil prices are continuing to push up with WTI crude up 2% to $95.70 on the day. In the equities space, we are seeing a more tepid mood with European indices falling off while US futures are sitting marginally lower on the day. German stocks are leading declines, with the mood music in Europe not helped by flagging PMI data which points to a Q2 economic contraction. As for major currencies, USD/JPY saw some volatile action after attempting to knock on the door of the 160.00 level. The currency pair fell from 159.95 to a low of 159.37 before quickly recovering back now to 159.80 levels. With traders continuing to poke and prod at the 160.00 mark, this will be an interesting one to watch in case we do see Japan decide to intervene once again to knock the pair down. Besides that, bonds and precious metals are once again under pressure as the status quo is prolonged. 10-year Treasury yields are back up to 4.48% while gold is down 0.5% to $4,463 on the day. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 头条新闻：美国总统特朗普重申伊朗已同意不拥有核武器；伊朗保留对任何允许美国发动攻击的国家进行自卫的权利；欧盟表示，美国以强迫劳动为由实施的最新关税措施缺乏依据；美元兑日元汇率持续试探干预区间；日本央行行长上田表示，若基本面展望保持不变，将持续上调政策利率；欧洲央行决策者埃尔德森表示，战争持久将增加第二轮 瑞士央行行长施莱格尔表示，中期通胀压力基本未变 受通胀压力激增影响，欧元区5月商业活动进一步承压 英国5月最终服务业PMI为49.3，初值为47.9 美国债务危机爆发的可能性有多大？市场：WTI原油上涨2%至95.70美元 欧洲股指走低，DAX下跌0.9%，CAC 40下跌0.4% 标普500期货下跌0.1% 美元小幅走高， 美元/日元波动加剧，此前曾逼近160关口 美国10年期国债收益率上涨2.8个基点至4.48% 黄金下跌0.5%至4,463美元 由于市场仍在等待美国与伊朗本周能否达成协议，今日交易氛围较为谨慎。但从目前情况看，双方似乎仍难以在关键条款上达成妥协。 美国总统特朗普出面重申，伊朗已同意不拥有核武器。但需要提醒的是，德黑兰方面上周早些时候就已经否认了这一所谓“基本承诺”。此外，他还表示，美国的海上封锁可能会持续到劳动节。如果真是这样，这意味着封锁还将再持续三个月，而解除海上封锁本应是伊朗在这项框架协议中的关键条件。对此，各位自行斟酌。尽管头条新闻喜忧参半，但市场整体仍未受影响。不过，油价持续攀升，西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）当日上涨2%，至95.70美元。在股市方面，市场情绪较为低迷，欧洲股指下跌，而美国股指期货当日小幅走低。德国股市领跌，疲软的PMI数据预示第二季度经济将出现萎缩，这进一步加剧了欧洲市场的低迷情绪。主要货币方面，美元兑日元在试图触及160.00关口后出现剧烈波动。该货币对从159.95跌至159.37的低点，随后迅速回升至159.80水平。 随着交易员们继续在160.00关口附近试探，如果日本真的决定再次干预以打压该货币对，这将是一场值得关注的博弈。此外，随着当前局势的持续，债券和贵金属再次面临压力。10年期国债收益率回升至4.48%，而黄金当日下跌0.5%，报4,463美元。本文由investinglive.com的Justin Low撰写。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Israel Lebanon ceasefire, Hezbollah permitting
items.summary: AI infrastructure spend on track to rival world's largest economies, Goldman says ICYMI - Kuwait targets 70% oil output recovery within weeks of Hormuz reopening RBA governor faces Senate grilling as CBA sees rates on hold after three straight hikes Australian April Exports leap higher at +7.2% m/m (vs. -2.7% prior) ANZ commodity index rises on tight supply, but NZD gains clip export returns PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8203 (vs. estimate at 6.7770) The Bitcoin puke continues. Back near $62K Trump told aides he would end Iran ceasefire if Tehran kills US troops, WSJ cite officials Trump official says US food supply, safety, not in danger from 'fully contained' screwworm Oil update, price a little lower. New Israel-Lebanon ceasefire hopes. Here we go again. Israel, Lebanon agree ceasefire framework, but Hezbollah holds the key. IEA stockpile warning and deeper crude draw add fuel to Wednesday's oil rally ICYMI - Fed dissenter Logan raises alarm on inflation ahead of Warsh's first meeting US House of Representatives votes against Trump's war. The vote means nothing. JPMorgan sees Strait reopening in June but leaves the hard question unanswered investingLive Americas market news wrap: Dollar firms as war angst creeps in Trump pumping the market after the close - says Iran pretty close to signing Broadcom shares slide after earnings Summary: The Trump administration announced Israel and Lebanon agreed to implement a ceasefire and establish security zones, contingent on a complete halt to Hezbollah fire and evacuation of its operatives from the South Litani sector; a previous ceasefire agreed last month had failed to hold Trump suggested a deal with Iran could come "over the weekend" while reports indicated he privately told aides he would not resume all-out war unless US troops are killed The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution to block further strikes on Iran, the first such vote of the conflict; analysts described it as largely symbolic Asian stocks fell Thursday morning, with Seoul and Tokyo both sharply lower as renewed Iran tensions drove a risk-off shift; the won fell sharply USD/JPY drifted back below 160; major FX moves were otherwise subdued Chinese analysts said PBoC liquidity would remain stable and ample in June despite the central bank skipping open market operations for a second day The New World screwworm, a parasitic fly lethal to warm-blooded animals, was found in a calf in Texas, raising alarm over the US cattle herd Oil prices edged lower on Wednesday as a flurry of contradictory signals on the US-Iran conflict left markets unable to commit to a direction, with Trump's weekend deal hint doing just enough to take the edge off what had been a firmly risk-off session. The day's dominant thread was a collision of escalation and diplomacy that has become the defining rhythm of this conflict. Iran struck Kuwait's international airport the previous day, injuring dozens and prompting US military strikes near the Strait of Hormuz. Against that backdrop, the Trump administration announced that Israel and Lebanon had agreed to implement a ceasefire and establish security zones in southern Lebanon, contingent on a complete cessation of Hezbollah fire and the full evacuation of its operatives from the South Litani sector. It was the second such ceasefire agreement in as many months; the first did not hold. Tehran has repeatedly linked any deal with Washington to an end of fighting on the Lebanese front, making the announcement diplomatically significant even if its durability remains an open question. Trump added to the noise in the US afternoon, suggesting a deal with Iran could materialise over the weekend. He was also reported to have told aides privately that he would not resume all-out war unless American troops were killed, a threshold that markets interpreted as confirmation the administration is tolerating ongoing skirmishing rather than seeking renewed escalation. The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution to block further Iran strikes, the first such vote since the conflict began in late February. The White House dismissed it and analysts broadly characterised it as performative, of no effect.. Asian markets opened Thursday in a risk-off mood. Seoul shares fell sharply and the Korean won sold off, while the Nikkei also declined, following a weak lead from Wall Street. Major currency moves were limited elsewhere, with USD/JPY drifting back below 160 by a few ticks without conviction in either direction. In China, analysts sought to soothe domestic market concerns, stating that liquidity conditions would remain stable and ample through June despite the People's Bank of China skipping open market operations again today, a communication exercise aimed at preventing the absence of intervention from being read as tightening. Away from geopolitics, the US Department of Agriculture confirmed the discovery of the New World screwworm in a Texas calf. The parasitic fly, which consumes warm-blooded animals alive, had been eradicated from the United States decades ago. Its reappearance raises serious questions about biosecurity and potential exposure across the American cattle herd at a time when beef prices are already near record highs. Still to come, RBA's Michele Bullock, Governor, Sarah Hunter, Assistant Governor (Economic), and Christopher Kent, Assistant Governor (Financial Markets) will all speak in Australia's parliament today. None will be dovish. due at 0500 GMT / 0100 US Eastern time Bitcoin continued to drop, hard, only finding a bounce under $62K: This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 高盛称，澳大利亚的AI基础设施支出有望与全球最大经济体比肩 错过了吗？——科威特计划在霍尔木兹海峡重开数周内将石油产量恢复至70% 澳大利亚储备银行行长面临参议院质询，澳大利亚联邦银行预计连续三次加息后利率将维持不变 澳大利亚4月出口环比大增7.2% （前值-2.7%）澳新银行大宗商品指数因供应紧张而上涨，但新西兰元升值削弱了出口收益 中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8203（市场预期为6.7770） 比特币暴跌仍在持续。重回6.2万美元关口附近 《华尔街日报》援引官员消息称，特朗普告诉助手，若德黑兰杀害美军，他将终止与伊朗的停火协议 特朗普政府官员称，美国食品供应和安全未受“已完全控制”的螺蛳蝇威胁 油市快讯，油价小幅走低。以色列与黎巴嫩停火重燃希望。历史重演。以色列、黎巴嫩达成停火框架，但真主党掌握关键。国际能源署库存警告及原油库存大幅下降助推周三油价上涨。ICYMI——美联储持异议的洛根在沃什首次会议前对通胀发出警告。美国众议院投票反对特朗普的战争。该投票毫无意义。 摩根大通预计马六甲海峡将于6月重开，但未解答棘手问题 investingLive 美洲市场新闻综述：随着战争担忧情绪蔓延，美元走强 特朗普收盘后提振市场——称伊朗已接近签署协议 博通股价在财报公布后下跌 摘要：特朗普政府宣布，以色列和黎巴嫩同意实施停火并建立安全区，但前提是真主党必须完全停止射击，并将其人员从南利塔尼地区撤离； 上月达成的停火协议未能维持 特朗普暗示与伊朗的协议可能在“本周末”达成，但有报道称他私下告诉助手，除非美军人员遇害，否则不会重启全面战争 美国众议院通过了一项战争权力决议案，旨在阻止对伊朗的进一步打击，这是冲突爆发以来的首次此类投票；分析师称此举主要具有象征意义 周四上午亚洲股市下跌，受伊朗局势再度紧张推动市场避险情绪升温，首尔和东京股市均大幅下挫； 韩元大幅贬值，美元兑日元汇率回落至160下方；其他主要外汇市场波动平缓；中国分析师表示，6月中国人民银行的流动性将保持稳定且充裕 尽管央行连续第二天暂停公开市场操作，但一种对温血动物致命的寄生蝇——新世界螺蝇， 这种对温血动物致命的寄生蝇，在得克萨斯州的一头小牛身上被发现，引发了对美国牛群的担忧。周三油价小幅走低，因美伊冲突传出的一系列矛盾信号令市场难以确定方向，特朗普周末关于达成协议的暗示，恰好足以缓解此前市场坚定避险的态势。当日的主旋律是升级与外交的碰撞，这已成为这场冲突的标志性节奏。前一天，伊朗袭击了科威特国际机场，造成数十人受伤，并引发了美国在霍尔木兹海峡附近发动军事打击。在此背景下，特朗普政府宣布，以色列和黎巴嫩已同意实施停火并在黎巴嫩南部建立安全区，但前提是真主党必须完全停止射击，并将其人员从南利塔尼地区全面撤离。这是两个月内达成的第二份此类停火协议；第一份协议未能维持。 德黑兰方面一再将与华盛顿达成的任何协议与结束黎巴嫩战线的战斗挂钩，因此，尽管该协议能否持久仍是个未知数，但这一声明在外交上具有重要意义。特朗普在美东时间下午又添了一把火，暗示本周末可能与伊朗达成协议。据报道，他还私下告诉助手，除非美军士兵阵亡，否则他不会重启全面战争。市场将这一门槛解读为，美国政府正在容忍当前的零星冲突，而非寻求新一轮升级。美国众议院通过了一项战争权力决议案，旨在阻止对伊朗的进一步打击，这是自2月下旬冲突爆发以来的首次此类投票。白宫对此不予理会，分析师普遍认为该决议仅具表演性质，并无实际效力。周四亚洲市场开盘时风险偏好低迷。受华尔街疲软走势影响，首尔股市大幅下挫，韩元遭抛售，日经指数亦出现下跌。其他主要货币波动有限，美元兑日元汇率小幅回落至160关口下方，市场对涨跌方向均缺乏明确信心。 在中国，分析师们试图安抚国内市场的担忧，表示尽管中国人民银行今天再次暂停公开市场操作，但流动性状况在6月前仍将保持稳定且充裕。此举旨在防止市场将未进行干预解读为货币政策收紧。抛开地缘政治因素，美国农业部证实，在得克萨斯州的一头小牛身上发现了新世界螺蛲蝇。这种寄生性蝇类会活生生吞食温血动物，几十年前已在美国被根除。在牛肉价格已接近历史高位之际，其卷土重来引发了人们对生物安全以及美国牛群可能面临感染风险的严重担忧。后续动态：澳大利亚储备银行（RBA）行长米歇尔·布洛克、经济助理行长莎拉·亨特以及金融市场助理行长克里斯托弗·肯特今日均将在澳大利亚议会发表讲话。他们均不会采取鸽派立场。预计格林尼治时间05:00 / 美国东部时间01:00发布。比特币持续大幅下跌，仅在6.2万美元下方出现反弹：本文由investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹撰写。

items.title: Eurozone business activity struggles further in May amid surging price pressures
items.summary: Services PMI 47.7 vs 46.4 prelim Prior 47.6 Composite PMI 48.5 vs 47.5 prelim Prior 48.8 Despite the troubles in France, the overall Eurozone services economy managed to hold up in May. However, private sector business activity as a whole fell to an 18-month low and marked back-to-back months of contraction for the first time since the end of 2024. Looking to the services sector, total new order inflows fell again for a third straight month. And while slowing from April, the pace of decline was nonetheless the second-sharpest since November 2024. On the prices front, the latest data showed a further intensification of inflationary pressures across the region. Input costs rose at the sharpest rate in three-and-half years, while charge inflation hit a 38-month high. Trouble, trouble. S&P Global notes that: "With business activity in the eurozone falling for a second successive month in May, it is looking increasingly likely that the economy will slip into contraction in the second quarter. The PMI data are indicating a 0.2% quarterly GDP decline barring any significant change in June. "Price pressures have meanwhile intensified to their most worrying for over three years, hinting at inflation potentially running close to 4% in the coming months. "These price pressures will sit uncomfortably with the ECB, which will want to be seen as acting swiftly to prevent higher inflation from becoming entrenched. However, policymakers will also clearly be concerned that they could be hiking rates into a downturn, adding to recession risks. "Hence, while one interest rate hike might be seen as an insurance policy, the case for further rate hikes will be harder to make if the economy continues to weaken, not least as this softening of demand will itself constrain pricing power and wage growth." This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 服务业PMI为47.7，初值为46.4，前值为47.6；综合PMI为48.5，初值为47.5，前值为48.8。尽管法国面临困境，但5月份欧元区服务业整体经济仍保持了稳定。然而，私营部门整体商业活动降至18个月低点，并自2024年底以来首次连续两个月出现收缩。从服务业来看，新订单总量连续第三个月下滑。尽管降幅较4月有所收窄，但仍是自2024年11月以来第二剧烈的下滑。在价格方面，最新数据显示该地区通胀压力进一步加剧。投入成本以三年半来最快的速度上涨，而收费通胀则创下38个月新高。麻烦，麻烦。标普全球指出：“随着欧元区5月份商业活动连续第二个月下滑，该经济体在第二季度陷入收缩的可能性正日益增大。除非6月份出现重大变化，否则PMI数据预示着季度GDP将下降0.2%。” “与此同时，物价压力已升至三年多来最令人担忧的水平，这预示着未来数月通胀率可能逼近4%。“这些物价压力将令欧洲央行感到不安，该行希望展现出迅速采取行动的姿态，以防止高通胀根深蒂固。然而，决策者显然也会担心，加息可能导致经济陷入衰退，从而加剧经济衰退的风险。“因此，虽然一次加息或许可被视为一种‘保险措施’，但如果经济持续走弱，进一步加息的理由将更难成立——尤其是因为需求疲软本身将抑制定价能力和工资增长。”本文作者为investinglive.com的贾斯汀·洛（Justin Low）。
items.summaryEn: Services PMI 47.7 vs 46.4 prelim Prior 47.6 Composite PMI 48.5 vs 47.5 prelim Prior 48.8 Despite the troubles in France, the overall Eurozone services economy managed to hold up in May. However, private sector business activity as a whole fell to an 18-month low and marked back-to-back months of contraction for the first time since the end of 2024. Looking to the services sector, total new order inflows fell again for a third straight month. And while slowing from April, the pace of decline was nonetheless the second-sharpest since November 2024. On the prices front, the latest data showed a further intensification of inflationary pressures across the region. Input costs rose at the sharpest rate in three-and-half years, while charge inflation hit a 38-month high. Trouble, trouble. S&P Global notes that: "With business activity in the eurozone falling for a second successive month in May, it is looking increasingly likely that the economy will slip into contraction in the second quarter. The PMI data are indicating a 0.2% quarterly GDP decline barring any significant change in June. "Price pressures have meanwhile intensified to their most worrying for over three years, hinting at inflation potentially running close to 4% in the coming months. "These price pressures will sit uncomfortably with the ECB, which will want to be seen as acting swiftly to prevent higher inflation from becoming entrenched. However, policymakers will also clearly be concerned that they could be hiking rates into a downturn, adding to recession risks. "Hence, while one interest rate hike might be seen as an insurance policy, the case for further rate hikes will be harder to make if the economy continues to weaken, not least as this softening of demand will itself constrain pricing power and wage growth." This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
